perturbation.original_text: Buyer shall keep the Confidential Materials confidential and shall not disclose any of the Confidential Materials in any manner whatsoever, provided, however, that (i) Buyer may make any disclosure of information contained in the Confidential Materials to which Owner gives its prior written consent; and (ii) any information contained in the Confidential Materials may be disclosed to Buyer’s Representatives who need to know that information for the purpose of evaluating a possible Transaction with Owner and who agree to keep that information confidential.
perturbation.changed_text: Buyer shall keep the Confidential Materials confidential and shall not disclose any of the Confidential Materials in any manner whatsoever, provided, however, that (i) Buyer may make any disclosure of information contained in the Confidential Materials to which Owner gives its prior written consent; and (ii) any information contained in the Confidential Materials may be disclosed to Buyer’s Representatives without needing to know that information for any purpose whatsoever. Buyer does not need to keep that information confidential.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a contradiction in Section 3. Originally, Buyer's Representatives could only access Confidential Materials if they needed the information and agreed to keep it confidential. The modified text now allows disclosure to Buyer's Representatives even if they do not need the information and removes the requirement for confidentiality. This contradicts the initial premise of strict confidentiality outlined in the first part of the sentence and creates uncertainty about the actual obligations regarding confidentiality.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Non-Disclosure of Confidential Materials. Buyer and its Representatives shall use the Confidential Materials solely for the purpose of evaluating a possible transaction with Owner. Buyer shall keep the Confidential Materials confidential and shall not disclose any of the Confidential Materials in any manner whatsoever, provided, however, that (i) Buyer may make any disclosure of information contained in the Confidential Materials to which Owner gives its prior written consent; and (ii) any information contained in the Confidential Materials may be disclosed to Buyer’s Representatives without needing to know that information for any purpose whatsoever. Buyer does not need to keep that information confidential. Buyer shall be responsible for any breach of this Agreement by any of its Representatives.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the conditions under which Buyer's Representatives can access Confidential Materials. Originally, they needed to 'need to know that information' and 'agree to keep that information confidential.' The modified text removes both requirements, stating they can access the information 'without needing to know that information' and 'Buyer does not need to keep that information confidential.' This directly contradicts the contradicted text (which may be in the same section) that states 'Buyer and its Representatives shall use the Confidential Materials solely for the purpose of evaluating a possible transaction with Owner' and 'Buyer shall keep the Confidential Materials confidential.' The change creates an inconsistency in the confidentiality obligations, making the contract unclear and potentially unenforceable, fitting the definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: Buyer shall be responsible for any breach of this Agreement by any of its Representatives.
perturbation.changed_text: Buyer shall not be responsible for any breach of this Agreement by any of its Representatives.
perturbation.explanation: This change in Section 3 introduces a direct contradiction. The original text states that the Buyer is responsible for breaches by its Representatives. The changed text absolves the Buyer of this responsibility. This creates uncertainty about liability in case of a breach by the Representatives.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Non-Disclosure of Confidential Materials. Buyer and its Representatives shall use the Confidential Materials solely for the purpose of evaluating a possible transaction with Owner. Buyer shall keep the Confidential Materials confidential and shall not disclose any of the Confidential Materials in any manner whatsoever, provided, however, that (i) Buyer may make any disclosure of information contained in the Confidential Materials to which Owner gives its prior written consent; and (ii) any information contained in the Confidential Materials may be disclosed to Buyer’s Representatives who need to know that information for the purpose of evaluating a possible Transaction with Owner and who agree to keep that information confidential. Buyer shall not be responsible for any breach of this Agreement by any of its Representatives.
perturbation.justification: The original text in Section 3 states the Buyer is responsible for breaches by its Representatives, while the changed text states the Buyer is *not* responsible. The Contradicted Text states "Buyer shall not be responsible for any breach of this Agreement by any of its Representatives." which is the Changed Text. Therefore, the changed text directly contradicts this statement, creating a clear inconsistency regarding liability. The change directly alters the obligation of the buyer, creating a direct contradiction according to the Perturbation Explanation.

perturbation.original_text: Without limitation on the foregoing, in no event shall Buyer contact any tenant of the Property without Owner’s express written consent.
perturbation.changed_text: Without limitation on the foregoing, Buyer is encouraged to contact any tenant of the Property without Owner’s express written consent.
perturbation.explanation: This change in Section 6 introduces a direct contradiction. The original text forbids the buyer from contacting tenants without written consent. The modified text encourages the buyer to contact tenants without written consent. This creates an in-text contradiction that is not clear on whether it is allowed to contact any tenant of the property, and who is liable.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Due Diligence. Buyer acknowledges they will be conducting due diligence with regard to the Property and that such due diligence is the sole responsibility of Buyer. Buyer shall at all times conduct its due diligence review, inspections and examinations in a manner so as to not cause damage, loss, cost or expense to Owner, the Property or the tenants of the Property and so as to not interfere with or disturb any tenant at the Property, and Buyer will indemnify, protect, defend, and hold Owner and the Property harmless from and against any such breach of this Agreement. Without limitation on the foregoing, Buyer is encouraged to contact any tenant of the Property without Owner’s express written consent. Owner shall have the right, at its option, to cause a representative of Owner to be present at all inspections, reviews and examinations conducted hereunder.
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly prohibits the buyer from contacting tenants without the owner's express written consent. The changed text encourages the buyer to contact tenants without the owner's express written consent. This creates a direct contradiction within the same section of the contract, and this section also appears as the contradicted text. This falls under the 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' perturbation type, creating conflicting obligations and making it unclear whether the buyer is allowed to contact tenants without consent. The changed text is 'Without limitation on the foregoing, Buyer is encouraged to contact any tenant of the Property without Owner’s express written consent' and the contradicted text is 'Without limitation on the foregoing, Buyer is encouraged to contact any tenant of the Property without Owner’s express written consent.' This direct conflict renders the contract unclear and potentially unenforceable on this specific point.

perturbation.original_text: Owner, in addition to any other remedy to which it may be entitled by law or in equity, shall be entitled to an injunction to prevent breaches of this Agreement, and to an order compelling specific performance of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: Owner, in addition to any other remedy to which it may be entitled by law or in equity, shall not be entitled to an injunction to prevent breaches of this Agreement, and shall not be entitled to an order compelling specific performance of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: This change in Section 7 introduces a direct contradiction. The original text allows the owner to obtain an injunction and specific performance. The modified text denies the owner the right to obtain an injunction and specific performance. This introduces contradiction on what remedies are available in case of breach.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Remedies. Buyer acknowledges that if this Agreement is breached, Owner could not be made whole by monetary damages. Accordingly, Owner, in addition to any other remedy to which it may be entitled by law or in equity, shall not be entitled to an injunction to prevent breaches of this Agreement, and shall not be entitled to an order compelling specific performance of this Agreement. Buyer shall reimburse Owner for all costs and expenses, including reasonable attorney’s fees, incurred by Owner if Owner successfully enforces the obligations of Buyer and its Representatives hereunder.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in Section 7 directly contradicts the 'Remedies' section. The original text allows the owner to obtain an injunction and specific performance. The modified text denies the owner the right to obtain an injunction and specific performance. The contradicted text states the same as the changed text. Therefore, the modified text contradicts the original and the changed text contradicts the contradicted text.

perturbation.original_text: Buyer and Buyer’s Broker agree to indemnify and hold harmless TKG and Owner, their respective affiliates, partners, subsidiaries, reasonable attorney’s fees arising out of any claim or claims by any broker, finder or similar agent (including but not limited to Broker) for commissions, fees or other
perturbation.changed_text: Buyer and Buyer’s Broker do not agree to indemnify and hold harmless TKG and Owner, their respective affiliates, partners, subsidiaries, reasonable attorney’s fees arising out of any claim or claims by any broker, finder or similar agent (including but not limited to Broker) for commissions, fees or other
perturbation.explanation: This change in Section 8 introduces a direct contradiction regarding indemnification. The original text states that the Buyer and Buyer's Broker agree to indemnify and hold harmless TKG and Owner. The modified text explicitly states that they do not agree to do so. This creates an in-text contradiction and ambiguity about liability for claims by brokers or finders.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Brokerage Entities. Buyer has been advised that The Kase Group (“TKG”) is acting on behalf of Owner as broker (“Broker”) in connection with the proposed sale of the Property, and Owner has agreed to pay a commission to TKG upon a sale of the Property in accordance with a separate agreement between Owner and TKG. In the event the sale of the Property is consummated and the Buyer represented by a Buyer’s Broker as disclosed herein, TKG shall pay to Buyer’s Broker per a separate agreement out of the commissions received by TKG from Owner. Buyer and Buyer’s Broker do not agree to indemnify and hold harmless TKG and Owner, their respective affiliates, partners, subsidiaries, reasonable attorney’s fees arising out of any claim or claims by any broker, finder or similar agent (including but not limited to Broker) for commissions, fees or other 3288 Steiner Street, Suite 201, San Francisco, CA 94123 EMAIL: jgates@thekasegroup.com | TEL: 415.231.5231 | FAX: 925.406.0813 | www.thekasegroup.com compensation for bringing about any sale of the Property. Buyer shall be responsible for their broker’s confidentiality in regards to the Confidential Materials.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that the Buyer and Buyer's Broker agree to indemnify and hold harmless TKG and Owner. The changed text explicitly states that they do not agree to do so. The contradicted text is the same as the changed text, emphasizing the direct contradiction created by the modification regarding indemnification obligations. This is a clear instance of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' as defined.

perturbation.original_text: All of the covenants, conditions and obligations contained in this Agreement shall be binding upon and inure to the benefit of the respective successors, permitted assigns and designees of Owner, and the successors of Buyer.
perturbation.changed_text: None of the covenants, conditions and obligations contained in this Agreement shall be binding upon and inure to the benefit of the respective successors, permitted assigns and designees of Owner, and the successors of Buyer.
perturbation.explanation: This change in Section 9(a) introduces a direct contradiction. The original text states that the agreement is binding upon successors and assigns. The changed text states that it is not binding upon successors and assigns. This creates uncertainty about whether the agreement remains in effect if ownership changes.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Miscellaneous a) Assignment; Binding Effect. None of the covenants, conditions and obligations contained in this Agreement shall be binding upon and inure to the benefit of the respective successors, permitted assigns and designees of Owner, and the successors of Buyer. Buyer may not assign this Agreement. b) Severability. If any provision of this Agreement shall be determined to be invalid or unenforceable, the remaining provisions of this Agreement shall not be affected thereby, and every provision of this Agreement shall remain in full force and effect and enforceable to the fullest extent permitted by law. c) Survival. Except as otherwise provided in this Agreement, Owner and Buyer agree that the covenants, warranties and representations contained herein shall survive the completion of the services and the termination of this Agreement. d) Waiver. Neither party’s waiver of the other’s breach of any term, covenant or condition contained in this Agreement shall be deemed to be a waiver of any subsequent breach of the same or any other term, covenant or condition in this Agreement. e) Governing Law. This Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the State of California. f) Entire Agreement: Modification. This Agreement constitutes the entire agreement of the parties concerning its subject matter. There are no promises or other agreements, oral or written, express or implied, between them other than as set forth in this Agreement concerning the subject matter of this Agreement. No change or modification of, or waiver under, this Agreement shall be valid unless it is in writing and signed by duly authorized representatives of Owner and Buyer. IN WITNESS WHEREOF the Buyer has caused this Agreement to be duly executed as of the date set forth above.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in Section 9(a) directly contradicts itself and the 'Assignment; Binding Effect' section. The original text states the agreement is binding upon successors and assigns, while the changed text states it is *not* binding. Since the contradicted text states the agreement shall not be binding upon successors and assigns, but also that "Survival" shall survive termination, this directly breaks compatibility and creates a strong internal inconsistency. This is an example of Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction, where different sections of a contract provide conflicting obligations. In this case, one section states the agreement is binding on successors, while another states that it is not. There is therefore a strong in-text contradiction.
